title: Lucid Reports Better-Than-Expected Q3 Results; Gravity SUV on Track for Late 2024 Production
text: Lucid exceeded expectations with Q3 2024 revenue of $200 million, surpassing the estimate of $196.3 million. The company reported an adjusted loss per share of $0.28, narrower than the anticipated $0.31 loss. Adjusted EBITDA came in at a loss of $613.1 million versus the expected loss of $634.4 million. The automaker produced 1,805 vehicles and delivered 2,781 units during the quarter. Management confirmed the company remained positioned to achieve its 9,000-vehicle annual production target. Lucid ended Q3 with $5.16 billion in cash, supplemented by an additional $1.75 billion raised through a share offering. Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund maintained its 58.8% ownership stake during the capital raise. CEO Peter Rawlinson stated the company's momentum continues with its third consecutive quarter of record deliveries. The company opened pre-orders for the Gravity SUV and confirmed production would commence before year-end 2024. The vehicle would start around $80,000, positioning it within federal EV tax credit eligibility thresholds. Lucid shares rose 3% on the earnings announcement.
